title: Three Reasons Why Dimensional Models Stand Out from the Pack | Dimensional
gist: Dimensional makes the evidence-based case for its multi-asset Models on three pillars: a financial-science (not forecasting) process targeting size/value/profitability in equities and term/credit/currency in fixed income; a long-run outperformance track record (78% of its US funds beat benchmarks over 20 years vs 16% industry-wide, 100% fund survivorship vs 45%); and low model-level turnover paired with daily fund-level implementation for cost and tax efficiency. This is a promotional/educational piece, but it carries hard performance and factor-framework data points.

insights.claim: Dimensional's investment process relies on information already embedded in market prices and empirical company data rather than market forecasts, deliberately rejecting backtest-chasing and hot-topic speculation.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Frames Dimensional's edge as discipline against forecasting error; an allocator should judge it on systematic factor capture, not tactical calls.
insights.quote: Rather than focusing on what's shined in a backtest or what is the hot topic today, Dimensional's investment approach starts with a belief in market prices
insights.locator: Section 1, 'The investment process is powered by financial science'

insights.claim: Within equities Dimensional overweights securities with smaller market capitalization, lower relative price, and higher profitability; within fixed income it targets higher expected returns along the term, credit, and currency-of-issuance dimensions.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Spells out the exact factor tilts an investor buys into via Dimensional; useful for mapping overlap and risk against other factor sleeves.
insights.quote: we design portfolios to overweight securities with smaller market capitalization, lower relative price, and higher profitability. Within fixed income, we target higher expected returns along the term, credit, and currency of issuance dimensions.

insights.claim: Dimensional warns that many managers claim 'factor-based' approaches, but factors identified in historical data do not necessarily translate into future outperformance, distinguishing truly evidence-based factors from spurious data-mined correlations.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: A diligence filter for screening smart-beta/factor products: demand out-of-sample, theory-backed evidence rather than backtested factor labels.
insights.quote: it's worth asking whether those factors are truly evidence-based and likely to appear in the future, or merely the product of chasing trends and spurious perceived correlations between data and returns.

insights.claim: Over the 20 years ending March 2026, 78% of Dimensional's US-domiciled funds outperformed their benchmarks, versus just 16% for the industry.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: A standout long-horizon batting average; central evidence for allocating to Dimensional over typical active managers.
insights.quote: over the 20 years ending March 2026, 78% of Dimensional's US-domiciled funds outperformed their benchmarks compared to just 16% for the industry.

insights.claim: 100% of Dimensional's US-domiciled funds survived the 20-year period ending March 2026, versus only 45% survivorship across the industry, meaning Dimensional investors avoided fund-closure disruption.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Survivorship bias inflates industry success rates; Dimensional's full survivorship makes its outperformance figure more credible and lowers reinvestment-risk for holders.
insights.quote: While only 45% of funds in the industry survived the 20-year period, investors in Dimensional funds were unaffected by closures, as 100% of Dimensional funds are still in existence.

insights.claim: Dimensional's Global Equity, Global Allocation 60/40, and Global Allocation 25/75 Portfolios beat their prospectus benchmarks by an annualized 76, 64, and 46 basis points respectively since each fund's first full month.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Quantifies the net-of-fee premium Dimensional's multi-asset solutions have delivered; the more equity-heavy the model, the larger the realized edge.
insights.quote: have beaten their prospectus benchmarks by an annualized 76, 64, and 46 basis points (hundredths of a percentage point), respectively, since each fund's first full month.

insights.claim: A $1,000 investment in the Global Equity Portfolio starting January 2004 grew to $7,085 by March 2026 versus $6,071 for its benchmark, illustrating the compounding effect of a sub-1% annual premium over two decades.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Shows the cumulative dollar payoff of a modest, persistent factor premium; reinforces the long-horizon case for staying invested.
insights.quote: that's a difference in ending wealth of $7,085 compared to $6,071 for the benchmark as of March 2026

insights.claim: Dimensional argues a manager's full body of work across all funds launched is more informative than evaluating a single strategy, which matters especially for multi-fund models.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Argues against cherry-picking a manager's best fund; evaluate the lineup-wide hit rate to gauge repeatability.
insights.quote: We believe a manager's body of work across all funds launched gives you more information about that manager than evaluating a single strategy.

insights.claim: Dimensional Models are designed for low turnover at the model level, which lowers transaction costs and improves tax efficiency, while daily active implementation at the fund level still incorporates real-time market information.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Separates strategic stability (model) from tactical flexibility (fund), capturing market info without the tax/cost drag of high turnover.
insights.quote: Our daily process at the fund level means that our models can incorporate real-time market information within their portfolios and still have low turnover at the model level.

insights.claim: Dimensional frames the stock/bond split, global diversification, and emphasis on known return drivers as the most powerful levers for managing portfolio risk and return, explicitly rejecting short-term tactical allocation driven by unreliable forecasts.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Positions tactical market-timing allocation as a source of unnecessary turnover and cost; favors a stable strategic mix.
insights.quote: a short-term, tactical approach to asset allocation—often motivated by unreliable market forecasts—can cause unnecessary turnover.

insights.claim: Dimensional's Research group works directly with leading academics including multiple Nobel laureates such as Eugene Fama, Robert Merton, Douglas Diamond, Merton Miller, and Myron Scholes, who have held director, consultant, or resident-scientist roles at the firm.
insights.speaker: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Credibility marker tying the factor approach to peer-reviewed academic finance; differentiates from purely commercial smart-beta shops.
insights.quote: works closely with leading academics—including several Nobel laureates—to better understand markets and what drives returns.
insights.locator: Section 1 and Footnote 1, Nobel laureate roster

source_meta.author: Kaitlin Hendrix, CFA, Asset Allocation Research Director and Vice President
